content.text:
{Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.}{Psalm 119:105}
 {Projection is the simplest of all geometric operations.}{— Hermann Weyl}

The **projection** of a vector onto a subspace is the closest 
point in that subspace.

content.title: Projection onto a Vector
content.content:
The **orthogonal projection** of $\vect{y}$ onto nonzero 
$\vect{u}$ is:
\[
\proj_{\vect{u}}\vect{y} = \frac{\vect{y} \cdot \vect{u}}{\vect{u} \cdot \vect{u}}\vect{u}
\]

content.text:
The component of $\vect{y}$ perpendicular to $\vect{u}$ is 
$\vect{y} - \proj_{\vect{u}}\vect{y}$.

content.title: Orthogonal Decomposition
content.content:
Let $W$ be a subspace of $\R^n$. Every $\vect{y} \in \R^n$ can be 
written uniquely as:
\[
\vect{y} = \hat{\vect{y}} + \vect{z}
\]
where $\hat{\vect{y}} \in W$ and $\vect{z} \in W^\perp$.

The vector $\hat{\vect{y}} = \proj_W\vect{y}$ is the orthogonal 
projection of $\vect{y}$ onto $W$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Best Approximation
content.content:
$\proj_W\vect{y}$ is the closest vector in $W$ to $\vect{y}$:
\[
\norm{\vect{y} - \proj_W\vect{y}} \leq \norm{\vect{y} - \vect{w}} 
\quad \text{for all } \vect{w} \in W
\]
content.label: Theorem

marginNotes.text:
The path of the righteous is like 
the light of dawn, which shines brighter and brighter until full day.
